channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'House of the Dragon,' Season 3, Episode 1: Number one with a gullet
item.description: In the Season 3 premiere, a new dragon enters the chat and the Battle of the Gullet is in full swing.
item.content:encoded:
In the Season 3 premiere, a new dragon enters the chat and the Battle of the Gullet is in full swing.

(Image credit: Ollie Upton)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: B to the B to the B
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WYSO listener John Blakelock of Yellow Springs, Ohio along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WYSO listener John Blakelock of Yellow Springs, Ohio along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Adrian Chiarella's "Leviticus" shows that horror's final boys can find love
item.description: Homophobia lives, and stalks, in Adrian Chiarella's debut feature.
item.content:encoded: Homophobia lives, and stalks, in Adrian Chiarella's debut feature.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for June 20, 2026: With Not My Job guest Caro Claire Burke
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Caro Claire Burke and panelists Karen Chee, Peter Grosz, Shane O'Neill
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Caro Claire Burke and panelists Karen Chee, Peter Grosz, Shane O'Neill

(Image credit: Jenn Udoni)

item.title: What makes a good dad joke? Psychology professor Paul Silvia shares the key pillars
item.description: What's brown and sticky? A stick, of course. NPR's Scott Simon talks to Paul Silvia, a psychology professor at UNC Greensboro, about new research on what makes a good dad joke.
item.content:encoded: What's brown and sticky? A stick, of course. NPR's Scott Simon talks to Paul Silvia, a psychology professor at UNC Greensboro, about new research on what makes a good dad joke.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Judy Blume says she's done writing: '50 years is enough!'
item.description: Judy Blume wrote her last book more than a decade ago. At the Santa Fe International Literary Festival, NPR's Scott Simon talked to Blume about her long career and why she doesn't miss writing.
item.content:encoded:
Judy Blume wrote her last book more than a decade ago. At the Santa Fe International Literary Festival, NPR's Scott Simon talked to Blume about her long career and why she doesn't miss writing.

(Image credit: Tira Howard Photography.)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: This couple got married at the end of Romeo and Juliet — and so will 31 others
item.description: At Free Shakespeare in the Park in New York, real weddings are happening every night after a production of Romeo and Juliet. But don't those characters die?
item.content:encoded:
At Free Shakespeare in the Park in New York, real weddings are happening every night after a production of Romeo and Juliet. But don't those characters die?

(Image credit: Rebecca J Michelson)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: James Burrows, director of classic shows 'Cheers' and 'Friends,' dies at 85
item.description: Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.
item.content:encoded:
Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.

(Image credit: Jason LaVeris)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Writer David Sedaris says there's a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is
item.description: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Writer David Sedaris talks about a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Writer David Sedaris talks about a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is.

item.title: Algae clouded Trump's vision for the Reflecting Pool. But scientists aren't surprised
item.description: The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.
item.content:encoded:
The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
